title: samolus valerandi var. floribundus
intro: Samolus valerandi var. floribundus, also known as Rock Whitlow-grass or Sea Whitlow-grass, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Primulaceae family. It is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, and its medicinal properties have been used to treat various health issues. The plant is rich in flavonoids, saponins, and other bioactive compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures that can be used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and other health issues.
